name: braid
description: Node-based canvas for Claude Code conversations: boards, branching, dedupe-merge, collapsed summaries, and compaction.

enumDescriptions: Inherit (unset; defer to the CLI/.claude default)
braid.model.markdownDescription: Model used for the main conversation. Empty = inherit the CLI/`.claude` default. (Does not affect collapsed summaries — those always use Haiku to save cost.)

enumDescriptions: high: deep reasoning (default for most models)
max: highest (only some models, e.g. Opus 4.6/4.7, Sonnet 4.6)
braid.effort.markdownDescription: Thinking effort: controls how much reasoning Claude invests. Works with adaptive thinking. **Only takes effect on models that support it**; others silently downgrade/ignore it. Empty = inherit the default.

enumDescriptions: Inherit (unset; defer to the model/.claude default)
On: adaptive thinking (Claude decides when and how much to think)
braid.thinking.markdownDescription: Extended-thinking switch. `adaptive` = enable adaptive thinking; `disabled` = off. **Adaptive only takes effect on supported models.** Inherit defers to the default. Works with Effort: under adaptive thinking, effort guides the depth.

enumDescriptions: default: prompt for approval before risky actions
plan: planning mode, no actual tool execution
braid.permissionMode.markdownDescription: Tool permission mode. ⚠️ **This extension has no permission-approval UI yet**: with modes that require approval (`default`/`plan`, etc.), tools needing approval are denied or hang. Default `bypassPermissions` (auto-skips approval, same as before). `inherit` defers to `permissions.defaultMode` in `.claude/settings.json`.

braid.maxTurns.markdownDescription: Max agentic turns per conversation round. `0` = unlimited.

braid.appendSystemPrompt.markdownDescription: Extra instructions appended after Claude Code's default system prompt. Empty = none.

braid.env.markdownDescription: Extra environment variables passed to the Claude Code subprocess. ⚠️ Do NOT set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` here — that switches the SDK to metered API billing instead of your subscription.

braid.autoCompactEnabled.markdownDescription: Match the official auto-compact: when a turn ends and the chain's context usage crosses the threshold, automatically compress that lineage into a compact node (reusing the "🗜 Compact here" mechanism). The threshold is high and rarely hit in normal use; turn off to only show the usage percentage and compact manually via 🗜.

braid.autoCompactThreshold.markdownDescription: auto-compact trigger threshold (percent of the model's context window). Default `95` (≈ the official level). Only applies when `autoCompactEnabled` is on. The mechanism reads the model's real window size, so it adapts to both 200K and 1M windows.

braid.expandAncestorsOnSelect.markdownDescription: When you select a board, also expand its whole parent lineage (ancestors) to the full detail card — a fisheye that shows the conversation you're on. Turn off to expand only the selected board itself (its ancestors stay compact). Idle compose boards always render in detail regardless.

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with Braid
walkthroughs.description: A node-based Claude Code canvas. Get set up in three steps, then tour the core features: branch, dedupe-merge, summaries, and compaction.

steps.title: Welcome to Braid
steps.description:
**Braid** turns your Claude Code conversations from a linear log into a **node canvas**: each Q&A round = one board, which you can branch to explore, multi-select to merge & dedupe, and collapse to see a summary.
Three steps to start: ① confirm subscription auth → ② one-click self-check → ③ open a canvas.

media.altText: Braid node canvas illustration: a root conversation branches into several branches, then dedupe-merges

steps.title: Confirm subscription auth
steps.description:
Just reuse the Claude subscription credentials already signed in on your machine — no key to enter in the extension.
**Important**: do not set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, or billing switches from your subscription to the metered API.

steps.title: Environment check / test connection
steps.description:
Click the button below to run a self-check and confirm the subscription-auth path works.
[Run environment check](command:braid.checkEnvironment)

steps.title: Open your first canvas
steps.description:
All set! Open a canvas and start your first round.
[Open canvas](command:braid.open)

steps.title: Ask & branch (⑂)
steps.description:
Type a question in a board's input box — the answer streams in live.
When it finishes, click **⑂ Branch** to spin off a child conversation. The original board is untouched, and the branch inherits its full context (`resume` + fork session).

media.altText: A board with a question and streaming answer, branching into a child board via the Branch button

steps.title: Merge & dedupe — the moat
steps.description:
Box-select two or more boards, then **⚡ Merge → new conversation**.
Braid walks each branch's ancestors, **sends the shared context only once**, and seeds a fresh chat with the deduped union — no copy-pasting, no confused model.

media.altText: A root chat branching into Branch A and Branch B, both selected, merging into a new deduped chat

steps.title: Summaries, zoom & compact (🗜)
steps.description:
**Collapse** a board to read an auto-generated structured summary; **zoom out** and it shrinks to a one-line mini summary.
Each board's badge shows the chain's **context-window %**, and **🗜** compacts a long chain into a single node. Select a board and press **Delete** to remove it.

media.altText: The same board shown expanded with a context badge, collapsed as a structured summary, zoomed out as a mini, and as a compacted node

steps.title: Cheat sheet
steps.description: All of Braid's core capabilities — ask, branch, dedupe-merge, summaries, context compaction, delete — in one quick reference on the right.
